questions.question:
Access to application resources in namespace `rbac-lab` is misconfigured.

A ServiceAccount named `report-reader` already exists, and a Pod `rbac-check` is available for validation.

Fix the RBAC configuration so that `report-reader` can:
- `get` Pods in namespace `rbac-lab`
- `list` Pods in namespace `rbac-lab`

The account must not receive broader permissions than required.

Do not grant cluster-admin, do not use a ClusterRoleBinding for this task, and keep the scope limited to namespace `rbac-lab`.

verification.description: A namespace-scoped Role and RoleBinding exist for report-reader
report-reader can get and list Pods in rbac-lab
report-reader is not over-privileged beyond the required read access

questions.question:
A Deployment named `web-app` already exists in namespace `rollout-lab`.

Perform a controlled rollout and rollback.

Requirements:
- update the image to `nginx:1.25.5`
- configure the rolling update strategy with `maxUnavailable=1` and `maxSurge=1`
- record the change so rollout history is visible
- save the output of `kubectl rollout history deployment/web-app -n rollout-lab` to `/tmp/exam/q202/rollout-history.txt`
- roll back the Deployment to the previous revision

The final running image must be the original pre-update image after the rollback completes.

verification.description: Deployment strategy is configured for a controlled rolling update
Rollout history output is saved to the expected file
Deployment was rolled back and is healthy on the original image

questions.question:
Traffic policy in namespace `netpol-lab` is too permissive.

The namespace contains Pods named `frontend`, `api`, and `db`.

Create or fix NetworkPolicies so that:
- `frontend` can reach `api` on TCP port `8080`
- `api` can reach `db` on TCP port `5432` using normal cluster DNS/service discovery
- direct traffic from `frontend` to `db` is denied
- unrelated ingress to `api` and `db` is denied by default

Do not modify the Pods or Services. Fix the policy layer only.

verification.description: NetworkPolicies exist, select the intended Pods, and preserve DNS egress for api lookups
Allowed paths frontend->api and api->db work on the required ports

questions.question:
Persistent storage for an application in namespace `storage-lab` is not binding correctly.

A PersistentVolume `app-data-pv`, a PersistentVolumeClaim `app-data`, and a Deployment `reporting-app` already exist.

Diagnose and fix the storage configuration so that:
- `app-data` is bound successfully
- `reporting-app` becomes Available
- the application Pod mounts the claim at `/data`

Do not recreate the application Deployment unless it is strictly required to apply the corrected storage configuration.

verification.description: reporting-app is Available and mounts the claim at /data
Storage fix preserves the intended PV/PVC relationship without orphaning resources

questions.question:
A Deployment named `metrics-agent` in namespace `scheduling-lab` is not scheduling onto the intended node pool.

Update the workload so that:
- it tolerates the taint `dedicated=ops:NoSchedule`
- it targets nodes labeled `workload=ops`
- the Pod becomes Running on a matching node

Do not remove the taint from the node. Fix the workload scheduling configuration instead.

verification.description: Deployment includes the required toleration and node selection rules
metrics-agent Pod is Running on a node labeled workload=ops
Scheduling fix keeps the workload constrained to the intended ops node pool
